actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour on this GameObject matches script Fire (m_Script fileID 11500000, guid 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d). Scene-configured fields: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, totalHitTime=5, maxHitInterval=2, neighbors=[], mainEffectGo=fileID 1849464941111748095. To cover ignition path: Ensure onNeighbors >= 2 via neighbor notifications (FindNeighbors/NotifyNeighborState from FireController or other system). Once onNeighbors >= threshold, this.shouldTurnOn becomes true; after timeToOn elapses (random in [40,60], set in Awake), UpdateFireState(true) should be invoked internally, pSystem.Play() called, and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1) executed.

Extinguish path under water: while fire is on, repeatedly cause IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) to be called at intervals < maxHitInterval (2s) until cumulative currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s). This sets isTurningOff=true, keeps resetting currentHitInterval, and after totalHitTime the script turns fire off, calls UpdateFireState(true) to stop particle systems, and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: The Fire script exposes no zero-parameter public methods; WaterHit requires a parameter and must be invoked by the water system or test harness.
